themes.description: Posts on Patristic Textual Evidence in relation to textual criticism, scribal changes, variants, and manuscript evidence.

themes.description: Posts on Paul and Crucifixion in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation.

themes.description: Posts on Paul and Jewish Law in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation.

themes.name: Paul and Opponents
themes.description: Posts on Paul and Opponents in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation.

themes.name: Paul and Persecution
themes.description: Posts on Paul and Persecution in relation to persecution, martyrdom, and Christian suffering under opposition.

themes.name: Paul and Resurrection
themes.description: Posts on Paul and Resurrection in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation.

themes.name: Paul and Women
themes.description: Posts on Paul's views of women, women associated with Paul, women apostles, interpolation debates, and gender roles in Pauline communities.

themes.name: Paul in Acts
themes.description: Posts on Paul in Acts in relation to Acts and early Christian origins and Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation.

themes.name: Paul's Knowledge of Jesus
themes.description: Posts on Paul's Knowledge of Jesus in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation.

themes.name: Pauline Apocalypticism
themes.description: Posts on Paul's end-time expectations, including Jesus' return, resurrection, the Spirit, the delay of the end, and later rapture interpretations of Paul's letters.

themes.name: Pauline Authorship
themes.description: Posts on which letters Paul wrote, which letters are disputed, and how scholars use style, theology, historical setting, and comparison with undisputed letters to assess authorship.

themes.name: Pauline Biography
themes.description: Posts on Pauline Biography in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation.

themes.name: Pauline Communities
themes.description: Posts on Pauline Communities in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation.

themes.name: Pauline Conversion
themes.description: Posts on Paul's conversion, its historical circumstances, and how it shaped his mission, theology, and understanding of Gentile inclusion.

themes.name: Pauline Ethics
themes.description: Posts on Paul's ethical teachings, especially slavery, Philemon, Onesimus, and moral questions in Pauline letters.

themes.name: Pauline Forgeries
themes.description: Posts on letters falsely attributed to Paul, including disputed Pauline letters, pseudepigraphy, motives for forgery, and debates over authenticity.

themes.name: Pauline Letter Composition
themes.description: Posts on how Pauline letters were composed, edited, combined, or transmitted, especially questions about 2 Corinthians and Philippians.

themes.name: Pauline Letters
themes.description: Posts on Paul's letters as a group, including lost letters, individual epistles, interpretation, communities, and broader questions about Paul's correspondence.

themes.name: Pauline Salvation Models
themes.description: Posts on Pauline Salvation Models in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation and salvation, judgment, atonement, grace, and divine punishment.

themes.name: Pauline Textual Criticism
themes.description: Posts on Pauline Textual Criticism in relation to Paul, his letters, theology, communities, and later interpretation and textual criticism, scribal changes, variants, and manuscript evidence.

themes.name: Pentateuch
themes.description: Posts on the Torah/Pentateuch, including Moses, Exodus, Sinai, the law, contradictions, sources, and historical questions about its narratives.

themes.name: Persecution (General)
themes.description: Posts on Persecution as a broad topic in relation to persecution, martyrdom, and Christian suffering under opposition.

themes.name: Persecution in the Epistles
themes.description: Posts on persecution, suffering, opposition, and endurance as addressed in New Testament letters such as 1 Peter, Hebrews, and 1 Thessalonians.

themes.name: Personal Reflections
themes.description: Posts offering personal reflections on belief, doubt, ethics, suffering, public issues, holidays, or life experience.

themes.name: Petrine Forgeries
themes.description: Posts on writings attributed to Peter, including debates over 1 Peter, 2 Peter, secretaries, literacy, and other texts falsely or questionably connected with Peter.

themes.name: Philippians
themes.description: Posts on Philippians, including authorship, composition, textual problems, further reading, and the letter's place among Paul's writings.

themes.name: Philippians Christ Poem
themes.description: Posts on Philippians 2:6-11, including Christ's status, humility, exaltation, and possible preexistence or incarnation.

themes.name: Pontius Pilate
themes.description: Posts on Pontius Pilate in relation to major figures in the New Testament narratives.

themes.name: Popularizing Scholarship
themes.description: Posts on explaining biblical scholarship to wider audiences through articles, books, media, public responses, and accessible presentations of academic work.

themes.name: Predestination
themes.description: Posts on Predestination in relation to salvation, judgment, atonement, grace, and divine punishment.

themes.name: Problem of Suffering (General)
themes.description: Posts on suffering as a challenge to belief in an all-powerful and loving God, including biblical responses and personal reflections.

themes.name: Prophets
themes.description: Posts on Hebrew Bible prophets, including Isaiah, Hosea, Zechariah, Daniel, prophecy, suffering servant traditions, and prophetic interpretation.

themes.name: Proto-Gospel of James
themes.description: Posts on Proto-Gospel of James in relation to non-canonical Christian texts and apocryphal writings.

themes.name: Proto-Orthodox Textual Changes
themes.description: Posts on textual changes that appear to support emerging orthodox views, especially against heretical or alternative interpretations of Jesus and Christian doctrine.

themes.name: Purgatory
themes.description: Posts on purgatory and related ideas, including postmortem purification, unforgivable sin, degrees of punishment, Perpetua, and the historical emergence of purgatorial belief.

themes.name: Q Source
themes.description: Posts on Q as a proposed source behind Matthew and Luke in relation to the canonical Gospels and their interpretation.

themes.name: Quran Fragments
themes.description: Posts on Quran Fragments in relation to manuscripts, textual transmission, and Bible translation.

themes.name: Redaction Criticism
themes.description: Posts on redaction criticism, especially how Gospel writers shaped, edited, and interpreted inherited traditions for their own theological or literary purposes.

themes.name: Research and Publishing
themes.description: Posts on scholarly research projects, academic publishing, peer review, journals, reference works, and the practical work of producing scholarship.

themes.name: Resurrection Apologetics
themes.description: Posts on arguments for or against proving Jesus' resurrection, including debate claims, legal arguments, miracle reasoning, eyewitness claims, and apologetic defenses.

themes.name: Resurrection Appearances
themes.description: Posts on traditions that Jesus appeared after death, including appearances to disciples, women, Paul, the Twelve, the five hundred, and questions about visions or hallucinations.

themes.name: Resurrection of Jesus
themes.description: Posts on Jesus' resurrection broadly, including historical reconstruction, early Christian belief, miracle claims, tomb traditions, and how the resurrection was understood.

themes.name: Resurrection of the Dead
themes.description: Posts on future bodily resurrection, including Jewish origins, Daniel, Maccabean martyrdom, Paul, physical embodiment, personal identity, and resurrection as afterlife hope.

themes.name: Resurrection Theology
themes.description: Posts on theological interpretations of Jesus' resurrection, including messiahship, salvation, the beginning of the church, Isaiah 53, Trinity, and early Christian belief.

themes.name: Revelation
themes.description: Posts on the book of Revelation as an ancient apocalypse, including its authorship, historical setting, symbolism, violence, judgment, 666, afterlife imagery, and modern misreadings.

themes.name: Revelation Authorship
themes.description: Posts on who wrote Revelation, including the identity of John of Patmos and whether Revelation was written by the same author as the Gospel of John.

themes.name: Rich and Poor in Afterlife
themes.description: Posts on afterlife stories about wealth, poverty, reversal of fortunes, Lazarus and the rich man, and moral warnings about riches after death.

themes.name: Rise of Christianity
themes.description: Posts on how Christianity spread and became successful in the Roman world, including social, religious, political, and cultural explanations.

themes.name: Roman Crucifixion and Burial Practices
themes.description: Posts on Roman crucifixion and burial practices, including how crucifixion was carried out, whether victims were buried, and how this evidence bears on Jesus' burial.

themes.name: Roman Persecution
themes.description: Posts on Roman Persecution in relation to persecution, martyrdom, and Christian suffering under opposition.

themes.name: Roman World
themes.description: Posts on Roman political, legal, military, and cultural settings relevant to Jesus, Paul, early Christianity, and the spread of the Christian movement.

themes.name: Salvation
themes.description: Posts on Salvation in relation to salvation, judgment, atonement, grace, and divine punishment.

themes.name: Scholar Memorials
themes.description: Posts remembering scholars and public religious figures, including their work, influence, and significance for biblical or early Christian studies.

themes.name: Scribal Changes
themes.description: Posts on Scribal Changes in relation to textual criticism, scribal changes, variants, and manuscript evidence.

themes.name: Septuagint
themes.description: Posts on the Greek translation of Jewish scripture, including its origin, textual issues, translation traditions, and importance for early Jewish and Christian interpretation.

themes.name: Sethian Gnostics
themes.description: Posts on Sethian Gnostics, including their myths, teachings, relationship to the Gospel of Judas, and summaries from After the New Testament.

themes.name: Sexual and Reproductive Ethics
themes.description: Posts on biblical and modern debates about sexuality, reproduction, surrogacy, gender identity, premarital sex, and the status of the unborn.

themes.name: Signs in John
themes.description: Posts on Signs in John in relation to miracle stories, supernatural claims, healing, exorcism, and visions.

themes.name: Social Class in Antiquity
themes.description: Posts on class, status, labor, poverty, slavery, gender hierarchy, and social conditions in the ancient Mediterranean world.

themes.name: Son of Man
themes.description: Posts on the Son of Man sayings, including what the phrase meant, whether Jesus used it for himself, and its role in apocalyptic traditions.

themes.name: Source Criticism
themes.description: Posts on source criticism, including how scholars identify written sources, composite texts, Gospel relationships, and possible spliced or edited writings.

themes.name: Suffering and Loss of Faith
themes.description: Posts on Suffering and Loss of Faith in relation to modern faith, nonbelief, deconversion, and religious doubt and suffering, evil, theodicy, and biblical responses to pain.

themes.name: Synoptic Problem
themes.description: Posts on the literary relationships among Matthew, Mark, and Luke, including Markan priority, Q, source theories, and explanations for similarities and differences.

themes.name: Teaching and Courses
themes.description: Posts about teaching, courses, lectures, class assignments, online events, and ways biblical studies is presented to students or public audiences.

themes.name: Textbooks
themes.description: Posts about textbooks and introductory teaching materials, including how they are written, revised, illustrated, and used to teach biblical studies.

themes.name: Textual Criticism (General)
themes.description: Posts on Textual Criticism as a broad topic in relation to textual criticism, scribal changes, variants, and manuscript evidence.

themes.name: Textual Criticism Methods
themes.description: Posts on Textual Criticism Methods in relation to textual criticism, scribal changes, variants, and manuscript evidence.

themes.name: Textual Variants
themes.description: Posts on differences among manuscript copies, why variants matter, and how changed wording can affect interpretation of biblical texts.

themes.name: Translation Process
themes.description: Posts on Translation Process in relation to manuscripts, textual transmission, and Bible translation.

themes.name: Trial of Jesus
themes.description: Posts on Jesus' trial before Jewish leaders or Pilate, including the charge against him, Gospel portrayals, anti-Judaism, Pilate traditions, and later non-canonical expansions.

themes.name: Trinity
themes.description: Posts on the development of the doctrine of the Trinity, including the Father, Son, Spirit, Nicaea, biblical interpretation, and later Christian theology.

themes.name: Triumph of Christianity
themes.description: Posts about how Christianity spread, converted the Roman world, and became a dominant religion.

themes.name: Valentinian Gnostics
themes.description: Posts on Valentinian Christians, their interpretation of scripture, theology, Gospel of Philip, and disputes with other early Christian groups.

themes.name: Virgin Birth
themes.description: Posts on the virgin birth traditions, including Matthew and Luke, textual questions, adoptionist Christology, ancient parallels, and historical or theological interpretation.

themes.name: Visions
themes.description: Posts on Visions in relation to miracle stories, supernatural claims, healing, exorcism, and visions.

themes.name: Women in Early Christianity
themes.description: Posts on women's roles, leadership, silence, gender hierarchy, and social status in early Christian texts and communities.

themes.name: Writing and Publishing
themes.description: Posts about writing habits, research, book production, publishing, titles, agents, reviews, and bringing scholarship to readers.

themes.name: Zealot Hypothesis
themes.description: Posts on claims that Jesus supported armed resistance to Rome, including the temple incident, zealot interpretations, Roman rule, and Bart's critique of the hypothesis.
